Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in T790M-Positive Advanced NSCLC
Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in T790M-Positive Advanced NSCLC (AURA3 Trial)
Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in AURA3 Trial

Research: This research evaluates the central nervous system (CNS) efficacy of osimertinib versus platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in patients with EGFR T790M-positive advanced non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who experienced disease progression on prior EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor (TKI) therapy. The study represents the first comparative evidence of an EGFR-TKI demonstrating improved CNS penetration and activity against brain metastases.
Research: Analysis of CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in Patients With T790M-Positive Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Data From a Randomized Phase III Trial (AURA3).

prior EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy.", "key_findings": {"primary_efficacy": {"measurable_CNS_lesions": {"osimertinib_ORR": "70% (21/30 patients, 95% CI: 51%-85%)", "platinum_pemetrexed_ORR": "platinum_pemetrexed_ORR": "17% (7/41 patients, 95% CI: 7%-32%)", "odds_ratio": "3.24 (95% CI: 1.33-8.81, P=0.014)"}}, "secondary_efficacy": {"median_CNS_duration_of_response": {"osimertinib": "8.9 months (95% CI: 4.3 months to not calculable)", "platinum_pemetrexed": "5.7 months (95% CI: 4.4-5.7 months)"}, "median_CNS_PFS": {"osimertinib": "11.7 months", "platinum_pemetrexed": "5.6 months", "hazard_ratio": "0.32 (95% CI: 0.15-0.69, P=0.004)"}}}, "methodology": {"study_design": "Randomized, open-label, international phase III trial", "randomization": "2:1 ratio considered the preferred treatment for T790M-positive NSCLC patients with CNS metastases", "Superior CNS penetration compared to platinum-based chemotherapy", "Meaningful clinical benefit with 70% response rate in patients with measurable brain lesions"], "patient_selection": ["EGFR T790M-positive status confirmed after progression on first-line EGFR-TKI", "Patients with asymptomatic, stable vs 5.6 months)", "First randomized evidence supporting osimertinib for CNS disease control"]}, "safety_and_tolerability": "The abstract does not detail adverse events; however, osimertinib's safety These findings address a critical unmet need for EGFR-TKIs with superior CNS penetration and support osimertinib as the standard of care for T790M-positive patients with brain involvement.", "clinical_trial_registration": "ClinicalTrials.gov NCT02151981"}

Research: Analysis of CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in Patients With T790M-Positive Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Data From a Randomized Phase III Trial (AURA3).
Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in AURA3 Trial

Research: This phase III randomized trial (AURA3) demonstrated superior central nervous system (CNS) efficacy of osimertinib compared to platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in patients with EGFR T790M-positive advanced non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who had progressed on prior EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy. Osimertinib achieved significantly higher CNS objective response rates (70% vs 31% in measurable lesions; 40% vs 17% in all CNS lesions), longer duration of response (8.9 vs 5.7 months), and prolonged CNS progression-free survival (11.7 vs 5.6 months, HR=0.32). These findings establish osimertinib as the preferred treatment for EGFR-mutant NSCLC patients with T790M mutations and CNS metastases.

Research: The AURA3 phase III trial provides the first comparative evidence demonstrating that osimertinib has superior CNS efficacy compared to platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in patients with EGFR T790M-positive advanced NSCLC and baseline CNS metastases. Osimertinib achieved significantly higher CNS objective response rates (70% vs 31% in measurable lesions; 40% vs 17% in all CNS lesions), longer duration of response (8.9 vs 5.7 months), and significantly improved CNS progression-free survival (11.7 vs 5.6 months; HR 0.32). These findings support osimertinib as an effective treatment option with improved CNS penetration for this patient population, addressing a significant unmet need for EGFR-TKIs with superior CNS activity.

Research: The AURA3 trial provides the first comparative phase III evidence demonstrating that osimertinib has superior CNS activity compared to platinum-based chemotherapy in T790M-positive advanced NSCLC. In patients with measurable CNS lesions, osimertinib achieved a 70% CNS objective response rate versus 31% for platinum-pemetrexed (OR 5.13, p=0.015). CNS progression-free survival was more than doubled (11.7 vs 5.6 months, HR 0.32, p=0.004). These results establish osimertinib as the preferred treatment for EGFR T790M-positive NSCLC patients with CNS metastases, addressing a critical unmet need for therapies with adequate brain penetration.

Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in AURA3 Trial

Research: This paper reports the CNS (central nervous system) efficacy outcomes from the AURA3 phase III trial, comparing osimertinib versus platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in patients with EGFR T790M-positive advanced NSCLC who progressed after prior EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy. The study addressed a critical unmet need for treatments with improved CNS penetration in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

Research: The AURA3 trial represents a pivotal randomized phase III study that established the superior CNS efficacy of osimertinib compared to platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in patients with EGFR T790M-positive advanced non-small-cell lung cancer who had progressed on prior EGFR-tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy. The study demonstrated statistically significant improvements in CNS objective response rates, duration of response, and progression-free survival, marking osimertinib as the first targeted therapy to show comparative CNS activity in this patient population.

Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in T790M-Positive Advanced NSCLC (AURA3 Trial)
Research: Analysis of CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in Patients With T790M-Positive Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Data From a Randomized Phase III Trial (AURA3).

Research: The AURA3 phase III trial provides landmark evidence demonstrating that osimertinib significantly outperforms platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in treating CNS metastases in EGFR T790M-positive advanced NSCLC. The 70% CNS ORR versus 31% for chemotherapy (p=0.015) and more than doubling of CNS PFS (11.7 vs 5.6 months, HR 0.32, p=0.004) establish osimertinib as the preferred treatment for this patient population. These findings address the critical unmet need for EGFR-targeted therapies with effective CNS penetration and represent a paradigm shift in managing EGFR-mutant NSCLC with brain metastases.
Research: Analysis of CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in Patients With T790M-Positive Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer: Data From a Randomized Phase III Trial (AURA3).

Research: This phase III randomized controlled trial (AURA3) demonstrated that osimertinib significantly outperforms platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in treating CNS metastases in EGFR T790M-positive advanced NSCLC patients. Osimertinib achieved a 70% CNS objective response rate versus 31% for chemotherapy in patients with measurable CNS lesions, with a hazard ratio of 0.32 for CNS progression-free survival.

Research: The AURA3 trial CNS efficacy analysis provides the first comparative evidence that osimertinib significantly outperforms platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in treating brain metastases from T790M-positive NSCLC. With a 70% CNS ORR versus 31% for chemotherapy and an 11.7-month median CNS PFS compared to 5.6 months, osimertinib represents a paradigm shift in managing EGFR-mutant NSCLC with CNS involvement. The excellent CNS penetration of osimertinib addresses a critical unmet need, as brain metastases historically confer poor prognosis and limited treatment options in this population.

Research: ## Research Report: CNS Efficacy of Osimertinib in AURA3 Trial

Research: The AURA3 phase III trial demonstrated statistically significant and clinically meaningful superior CNS efficacy of osimertinib over platinum-pemetrexed chemotherapy in T790M-positive advanced NSCLC patients with brain metastases. Osimertinib achieved 70% CNS ORR versus 31% for chemotherapy in patients with measurable lesions, with a 68% reduction in CNS progression risk. These findings establish osimertinib as the preferred treatment option for EGFR T790M-positive patients with CNS involvement, addressing a critical unmet need for targeted therapies with effective CNS penetration.
